sutta: monks when a worthy man is born into a family it is for the good benefit and happiness of many folk it is for the good happiness and benefit of his parents of his wife and children of his slaves workmen and servants of his friends and companions of the ghosts of his own ancestors of the raja of the devas and of recluses and brahmanas monks jesus abundance of rain brings to perfection all crops for the good benefit and happiness of many folk even so a worthy man is born into a family for the good benefit and happiness of many folk it's the end of the supta so i stated here if you have a virtuous person a good person he can benefit many many people and it is mentioned here even the ghosts of his ancestors get the benefit of his meritorious deeds of his virtue and we are at the chapter of the aids in the fourth book of the young utani kaya before i go to the sutas i just like to mention i made 2 mistakes in pronunciation the last time the last song i mentioned i read in missile actually it should be nbc nbc is a full stupid fellow and earlier several talks earlier i pronounce the word mahut the spelling is m-a-h-o-u-t that's an elephant trainer it should be pronounced ma
